skill.name: chapter-outline-generator
skill.description: Generates comprehensive chapter outlines for books, including key topics, subtopics, learning objectives, and estimated word counts. Use this when the user needs help structuring a book chapter or creating a table of contents.
skill.summary: Creates structured chapter outlines with summaries, objectives, sections, subsections, and word count guidance.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by fenced markdown examples in SKILL.md. Static weak-cryptography findings are also false positives; the cited lines contain prose and planning prompts, not cryptographic code. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, data exfiltration, or executable script behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged fenced markdown blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited ranges are documentation examples that show the expected outline format and sample outputs, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is inside markdown code fences used to demonstrate output formatting. No shell command, Ruby code, subprocess call, or user-controlled execution path appears in the cited lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at lines that contain the skill description and outline-planning questions. These lines do not reference cryptographic algorithms, hashing functions, encryption, credentials, or security-sensitive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is plain writing guidance about book structure and target length. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptography or any crypto-related behavior.

content.value_statement: Authors often struggle to turn broad book ideas into balanced chapter plans. This skill creates clear outlines with summaries, objectives, sections, subsections, and estimated word counts.
actual_capabilities: Builds book chapter frameworks from title, genre, audience, theme, and length goals.
Creates chapter titles, short summaries, learning objectives, main sections, and subsections.
Estimates word counts based on requested book length and chapter importance.
Checks outline flow for gaps, redundancy, progression, and balanced coverage.
Provides example structures for fiction and nonfiction book projects.
limitations: Does not research facts, citations, market data, or comparable titles.
Does not write full chapters from the outline.
Needs user context for accurate genre, audience, length, and scope decisions.
May need revision for publisher-specific formats or academic requirements.

use_cases.title: Plan a nonfiction book
use_cases.description: Create a full chapter map with objectives, sections, and word counts before drafting.

use_cases.title: Structure early fiction chapters
use_cases.description: Organize opening chapters so character setup, conflict, pacing, and scene progression are clear.

use_cases.title: Rebalance an existing manuscript
use_cases.description: Review a current chapter list and adjust scope, order, and chapter length distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning from a simple book idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chapter outline for a book titled [title]. The genre is [genre], the audience is [audience], and the target length is [word count].

prompt_templates.title: Expand one chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a single chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Expand chapter [number or title] into a detailed outline with a summary, learning objectives, main sections, subsections, and estimated word count.

prompt_templates.title: Improve flow and balance
prompt_templates.scenario: Revise an existing table of contents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this chapter list for logical flow, missing topics, overlap, and word count balance. Suggest a stronger outline: [paste outline].

prompt_templates.title: Design a complete book plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a full manuscript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete chapter framework for [book concept]. Include chapter goals, reader takeaways, section hierarchy, pacing notes, and word count allocation.

output_examples.input: Outline the first three chapters of a mystery novel about art theft.
output_examples.output: A three-chapter opening plan with chapter titles, scene-focused sections, character introductions, clues, stakes, and estimated chapter lengths.

output_examples.input: Create a nonfiction outline for a book about remote team management.
output_examples.output: A business book structure with chapter summaries, leadership objectives, major management topics, supporting subsections, and balanced word counts.

output_examples.input: Rework my table of contents so the middle chapters feel less repetitive.
output_examples.output: A revised chapter order that merges overlapping material, fills topic gaps, and assigns clearer purposes to each chapter.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, book goal, genre, and approximate length before asking for an outline.
Review the outline as a whole before expanding any single chapter.
Ask for a second pass focused on gaps, redundancy, pacing, and word count balance.
anti_patterns: Requesting a complete outline without giving the subject, audience, or desired scope.
Treating the first outline as final without checking flow across chapters.
Using generic chapter titles when the project needs a specific voice or market position.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full table of contents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create a chapter-by-chapter table of contents with summaries and internal section structure.

faq.question: Does it work for fiction and nonfiction?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for fiction pacing and nonfiction learning objectives.

faq.question: Can it estimate chapter word counts?
faq.answer: Yes. It estimates word counts from the total book length and the relative importance of each chapter.

faq.question: Will it write the full manuscript?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning, structure, and chapter organization rather than drafting full chapters.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the title, genre, target audience, main theme, desired length, and preferred number of chapters if known.

faq.question: Can it revise an existing outline?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review an existing outline for logical order, gaps, overlap, and chapter balance.
